title: The Mischiefy Moles and the Bookcase of Chaos
summary: Two adventurous moles named Disco and Fisco set out to cause mischief in a child's bedroom. They scale a tall bookcase to reach the top shelves and accidentally knock over a lamp before heading to the dark space beneath a chair. Armed with a flashlight, they hide rusty old toys in their new lair. However, their pranks are quickly outstaged by the arrival of Mischief Mouse, who wreaks havoc by toppling the entire bookshelf and all the pictures, proving that there really is such a thing as too much trouble.

highlightQuote.text: I want to stand for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We put Silver's blanket on top of the basket and that worked pretty well, so let's try that again.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You did lots of that... that would make some stars...

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There. There we go. Let's see. Okay, now we'll turn out the light and we'll see. Oh yeah, that made some nice stars. Okay, you want to sit in the chair or on the floor?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I want to stand for stars and stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Let's tell our story.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First pack and plays, then we tuck in any mice, and then there's pack and plays right here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The way you do it is you open... where's the zipper?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, we should do that after stars and stories because it's a little dark to do that right now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Okay. I can leave it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, that's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, I like to sit in your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I love that. All right. Here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, I don't feel really right in your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well I was just moving so I could lean against the crib. I've got a good spot now if you want to sit.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No? Okay. Who should we tell a story about tonight?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Mischiefy Moles.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the Mischiefy Moles.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The Mischiefy Moles, Disco and Fisco.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Disco and Fisco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can do mischief in my closet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well once upon a time, Disco and Fisco had had a good breakfast.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Disco and Fisco, I have a little question.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can explore and do mischief in my closet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: We can? Thanks!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but there's not much you can do mischief with. And... but how about there's lots of things to do mischief with inside of my bookcase?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the bookcase! That sounds like fun.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And baskets.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, how about we let Finn in for one minute?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, Finn cat? Okay. You can stay in for one minute.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And close the door, but leave a little spot for Finn.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, yeah, so he can go out when he needs to. I'll give him some pets.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There. Now we can tell our story.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Fisco said, "Wow, this is exciting!" And right away they both started climbing up the shelves of the bookshelf. And they got up to the second shelf, puff puff, wow this is tough climbing, but there's lots of books up there we want to see. And then they got up to the third shelf, puff puff. And what book did they find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think they found any mole books, but they climbed up to the top and they found mischiefy things.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what kind of mischiefy things did they find?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They just climbed up and just went clonk, clonk, clonk, crash!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, did they fall down?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they knocked something off the top of the bookshelf? What did they knock off?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They knocked off our lamp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh my goodness, Disco and Fisco, what are you doing? And what did they say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mischief!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well that's not very nice because we need that lamp. I know it's fun to do mischief, but...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then they put back the lamp.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks for putting it back. That was nice of you. And they said, "Oh, sorry." Yeah, that's okay Disco and Fisco.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Moles, there's lots of things to do mischief with under the chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Under the chair!" said Disco and Fisco. "Let's try it!" and they ran down the bookshelf and they ran under the chair and they said, "Wow, uh, what's under here? I can't really see because it's too dark," said Disco. Well Fisco said, "I have an idea." What was Fisco's idea?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Disco, here, here's a light.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: "Oh yeah, here's a light. Let's bring it under the chair and see what we can see." And Disco said, "Wow, that's so bright! Oh yeah, now I can see." And what did they see under the chair?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They saw nothing.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: There was nothing? And then Fisco said, "Haha, I have an idea. We can put things under the chair that don't belong there. That's a kind of mischief."

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But no, not my toys.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, okay. We won't put your toys under there. What else could we put under there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You could put under there something from downstairs, like some of my old rusty things that I put in one big bin.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, some old toys?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, yes. They're even rusty. And you can put them under the chair.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So Disco and Fisco went downstairs and found some old rusty toys and brought them up and put them under the chair. And then what did they do? Let's do one more mischief place.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They mischiefed it. Then Mischief Mouse wandered along and she set them back up again and she knocked over all of my pictures, even the entire bookshelf.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Mischief Mouse knocked over the entire bookshelf? And Disco and Fisco said, "That's too much mischief, Mischief Mouse. What are you doing?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm mischief! I'm doing mischief!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, but that's not nice to knock over someone's bookshelf. We better fix it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can do more mischief than that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, what did you do this time?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now the story's over, but should I do some shadow puppets?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Chop, chop, an alligator.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you do shadow puppets at school at all? Probably not, huh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Ooh, a bird.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a bird shadow. Yeah. Oh, I can't see it, Izzy. You gotta hold your hands over the light a little bit more. There you go. Oh, hey, look up at the ceiling. And tada! Who's that? Hello, I'm Shadow Uncle Bill. Hi.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Fisco
characters.description: The second Mischiefy Mole, who has the idea to use a light and hide toys under a chair.

characters.name: Finn
characters.description: A resident of Cat World and loyal companion to Mama, Finncat is a meticulous research collector who documents the world through investigative sniffing. Alongside their peer Silver, they navigate their domain with scholarly curiosity, often pausing mid-voyage for a sudden, dramatic flop. While currently thwarted by the formidable challenge of an unopened food can, this diligent scholar occasionally graces the room for a fleeting minute during storytelling, lending a brief, discerning presence to the narrative before returning to their investigations.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Shadow Uncle Bill
characters.description: A shadow puppet character introduced at the end of the session.

places.name: The Closet
places.description: A secluded hollow Izzy suggested for the moles’ exploration, though she initially warned of its sparse interior; it has since become a clandestine cache where the pets squirrel away their plush fish and mouse toys.

places.name: The Bookcase
places.description: A tall structure the moles climb, leading to a knocked-over lamp.

places.name: Under the Chair
places.description: A shadowed subterranean vault where the moles secure the spoils of their morning forage, illuminating piles of jagged, rust-eaten toys with the singular, sweeping beam of a flashlight.

places.name: Downstairs
places.description: Ova’s birthday gala transformed the discarded bin of rusty toys into a sprawling patchwork of global wonders. Corroded tin trains served as the iron-clad mountains of a miniature Eurasia, while the oxidized gears of a forgotten wind-up soldier formed the jagged, red-dust canyons of a metallic Australia. Beneath the festive bunting, the weathered plastic and peeling paint of these childhood relics became the vibrant, rugged continents of a mouse’s vast and storied world.
